sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Roth Capital Reaffirms Buy Rating on AudioEye (AEYE) with Optimistic Outlook
feed.summary: Roth Capital has reaffirmed its Buy rating on AudioEye (AEYE) and set a $25 price target, following the company's positive preliminary Q4 results. Management anticipates strong growth and improved operating margins throughout 2026, which Roth Capital believes significantly reduces investment risks. Despite current financial vulnerabilities like a negative operating margin and high debt-to-equity ratio, the firm sees potential for a rebound given the stock's oversold status and significant insider ownership.

feed.title: Royal Caribbean Group (RCL) Trading Down 3.91% on Jan 13
feed.summary: Royal Caribbean Group (RCL) saw its shares decrease by 3.91% on January 13, trading at an intraday low of $289.05 before slightly recovering. Wall Street analysts maintain an "Outperform" status with an average target price of $327.76, while GuruFocus's GF Value suggests a potential downside of 30.63% from the current price. This article was generated using automated technology and GuruFocus financial data for timely market reporting.

feed.title: 44th Annual J.P. Morgan Healthcare Conference
feed.summary: Intellia Therapeutics announced its participation in the 44th Annual J.P. Morgan Healthcare Conference. The event is scheduled for January 14, 2026, at 9:00 AM PST, and a webcast will be available for remote attendance.

feed.title: Stock Market Live January 14, 2026: S&P 500 (SPY) Losing Ground on Earnings
feed.summary: The S&P 500 (SPY) is losing ground on January 14, 2026, driven by mixed earnings reports from banks like Wells Fargo and Bank of America. Key stock movements include Intel rising on an upgrade and L3Harris Technologies gaining after a Pentagon investment. Global tensions, particularly concerning Iran, are leading to speculation of higher oil and gold prices, with analysts predicting gold could reach $5,000.

feed.title: SciSparc Faces Nasdaq Non-Compliance Challenge Over Stockholders’ Equity
feed.summary: SciSparc Ltd. is facing a Nasdaq non-compliance notice regarding its stockholders' equity, leading to an 11.03% drop in stock value. The company's management remains optimistic, asserting that recent financial actions will help them meet the required fiscal benchmarks. Investors are advised to closely monitor SciSparc's efforts to regain compliance and stabilize its market presence.

feed.title: Adobe Inc (ADBE) Stock Price Down 3.58% on Jan 13
feed.summary: Shares of Adobe Inc (ADBE) fell 3.58% in mid-day trading on January 13, reaching an intraday low of $315.39. Despite the drop, analysts have an average target price of $428.07, suggesting a 35.50% upside, and the stock holds an "Outperform" status from brokerage firms. GuruFocus estimates a significantly higher GF Value of $663.42 for ADBE, indicating a 110% upside.

feed.title: ARE FRAUD NOTICE: BFA Law Notifies Alexandria Real Estate Equities, Inc. Investors that the Company was Sued for Fraud and to Contact the Firm by January 26 Class Action Deadline
feed.summary: Bleichmar Fonti & Auld LLP has filed a class action lawsuit against Alexandria Real Estate Equities, Inc. (NYSE: ARE) and its executives for securities fraud. The lawsuit alleges that the company misrepresented its leasing volume and development pipeline, particularly concerning a property in Long Island City, leading to a significant stock drop. Investors who purchased Alexandria Real Estate securities have until January 26, 2026, to seek appointment as lead plaintiff in the case.

feed.title: Kenvue stock edges up as Kimberly-Clark vote nears — the deal spread traders are watching
feed.summary: Kenvue shares rose slightly as its upcoming sale to Kimberly-Clark draws near, with investors focused on the closing deal spread. Kimberly-Clark submitted additional documents to U.S. regulators, and Kenvue shareholders are scheduled to vote on the cash-and-stock deal on January 29. The current Kenvue stock price is about 5% below the implied value of the agreed deal, reflecting market risks despite the CEOs' positive outlooks.

feed.title: Datavault AI Expands IBM Collaboration to Deploy Enterprise-Grade AI at the Edge with Available Infrastructure's SanQtum AI Platform
feed.summary: Datavault AI Inc. announced an expanded collaboration with IBM to deploy enterprise-grade AI at the edge in New York and Philadelphia using the SanQtum AI platform. This initiative will establish a secured multi-city edge AI network for real-time data tokenization, security, and monetization, leveraging IBM's watsonx portfolio on a zero-trust network. The system aims to provide ultra-low-latency data processing and monetization, transforming raw data into authenticated, tradable digital property without relying on public cloud infrastructure.

feed.title: Cardinal Health introduces ContinuCare™ Pathway; announces relationship with Publix
feed.summary: Cardinal Health has launched ContinuCare™ Pathway, a program designed to help retail pharmacies refer patients to Cardinal Health at-Home Solutions for rapid home delivery of diabetes supplies. Publix Super Markets Inc. has enrolled its entire pharmacy network of nearly 1,400 pharmacies in the program. This initiative aims to streamline the process for patients to receive medically necessary devices and supplies, particularly for those covered under complex insurance requirements.

feed.title: TD Cowen is Bullish on The Procter & Gamble Company (PG)
feed.summary: TD Cowen analyst Robert Moskow maintained a buy rating on The Procter & Gamble Company (PG) but trimmed its price objective from $168 to $150, anticipating challenges for large-cap consumer staples in 2026. Separately, the Texas Attorney General reached a settlement with P&G regarding accurate fluoride representation in Crest children's toothpaste marketing. P&G is a major consumer product maker, though the article suggests certain AI stocks may offer greater upside.

feed.title: Gevo awarded patent for ethanol-to-olefins process to produce renewable jet fuel (GEVO:NASDAQ)
feed.summary: Gevo has been awarded a patent for its ethanol-to-olefins technology, which is used to produce renewable jet fuel and chemicals. This patented process is expected to reduce operating costs by up to 35% compared to existing technologies. The news led to a 2.2% increase in Gevo's stock during Wednesday's trading.

feed.title: DoorDash, Uber Drivers Stiffed on $550 Million in Tips, NYC Says
feed.summary: New York City alleges that DoorDash and Uber Technologies have deprived delivery workers of over $550 million in tips. This deprivation reportedly occurred after the companies altered their app interfaces, moving tipping prompts to less prominent locations following the implementation of new pay standards in December 2023. The city claims this was done to make upfront delivery costs appear lower, as service fees were raised to subsidize the new pay rates.

feed.title: Coty Inc. (COTY) exits Wella in a $750 million agreement with KKR
feed.summary: Coty Inc. has announced its complete exit from Wella through a $750 million agreement with KKR. This move is part of Coty's strategy to simplify its portfolio and focus on its core businesses, significantly reducing its debt. The deal represents a positive step for Coty as it continues its transformation.

feed.title: Chevron’s Price Target Upped At Jefferies On Higher Earnings Forecast
feed.summary: Jefferies has increased Chevron's price target to $189 from $174 and maintained a 'Buy' rating due to expectations of higher earnings. The firm predicts adjusted earnings per share of $1.45 for Q4 2025, which is about 2% above consensus. Investors are advised to watch developments in Venezuela, TCO production in Kazakhstan, updates on the El Segundo fire, and the gas-to-AI outlook.

feed.title: The Zacks Analyst Blog Highlights Royal Caribbean Cruises, Carnival and Norwegian Cruise Line
feed.summary: The Zacks Analyst Blog highlights Royal Caribbean Cruises (RCL), Carnival Corp. (CCL), and Norwegian Cruise Line (NCLH) as top picks in the cruise industry for 2026, driven by strong demand and booking trends. The article details each company's financial outlook, including projected revenue and earnings growth, and discusses strategic investments and positive analyst revisions, suggesting strong near-term upside.

feed.title: Wells Fargo Raises Price Target for EPAM Systems to $247 | EPAM Stock News
feed.summary: Wells Fargo has increased its price target for EPAM Systems (EPAM) to $247 from $185, maintaining an "Overweight" rating. This significant 33.51% increase reflects a strong positive outlook from analyst Jason Kupferberg. The average target price from 17 analysts is $217.57, with a consensus "Outperform" status, while GuruFocus estimates a one-year GF Value of $305.18.

feed.title: Dollar Tree stock hits 52-week high at $140.0 By Investing.com
feed.summary: Dollar Tree Inc. (DLTR) share price reached a new 52-week high of $140.0, marking a 126% increase from its low and reflecting a 98.95% surge over the past year. This performance is attributed to strategic initiatives like share buybacks and strong market position, despite some analysts having varied outlooks. The company also recently appointed Daniel Delrosario as Senior Vice President of Investor Relations and Treasurer.

feed.title: BofA Securities Adjusts Vulcan Materials Price Target to $348 From $305, Maintains Buy Rating
feed.summary: BofA Securities has raised its price target for Vulcan Materials (VMC) to $348 from $305, while reiterating a Buy rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects an updated outlook on the construction materials company. The article also lists other recent analyst activities, including downgrades and price target changes from various firms.

feed.title: BofA Securities Adjusts United Rentals Price Target to $1,050 From $1,079, Maintains Buy Rating
feed.summary: BofA Securities has revised its price target for United Rentals (URI) to $1,050, down from the previous $1,079. Despite this adjustment, the firm has chosen to maintain its "Buy" rating for the equipment rental company. This indicates ongoing positive sentiment towards United Rentals, even with a slight reduction in the target valuation.

feed.title: Archer and SLB Win Equinor Deepwater P&A Contract in Gulf of America
feed.summary: Archer, in collaboration with SLB, has secured an integrated plug and abandonment contract from Equinor for three deepwater wells on the Titan platform in the Gulf of America. This award highlights the combined strengths of both companies and reinforces Archer's position in the expanding deepwater P&A market. The contract covers comprehensive project management, engineering, and various P&A technologies.

feed.title: Pfizer Completes Key China Phase 3 Trial for Migraine Drug Rimegepant
feed.summary: Pfizer Inc. has completed a Phase 3 clinical trial in China for its migraine prevention drug, rimegepant. The study aimed to assess the efficacy and safety of rimegepant for migraine prevention in Chinese adults who had not previously used migraine medications. This marks a significant step for Pfizer to expand its post-pandemic drug portfolio into the competitive Chinese migraine market.

feed.title: The Bull Case For Royal Caribbean (RCL) Could Change Following PortMiami Terminal G Groundbreaking – Learn Why
feed.summary: Royal Caribbean Group recently broke ground on a new US$345 million, LEED-targeted Cruise Terminal G at PortMiami, designed to accommodate large Icon-class ships and reinforce Miami's role as a global cruise hub. This investment supports Royal Caribbean's long-term capacity expansion alongside its shipbuilding agreements, but its success hinges on sustained strong demand and pricing power. The article notes a wide range of fair value estimates for RCL and highlights the importance of monitoring consumer discretionary spending.

feed.title: Assessing Vital Farms (VITL) Valuation After Reduced 2025 Guidance And Mixed Analyst Target Resets
feed.summary: Vital Farms (VITL) saw its share price decline following reduced 2025 revenue guidance, despite analysts highlighting resilient earnings fundamentals. The stock is currently trading at US$30.59, significantly below the average analyst target and intrinsic value estimate of US$49.45, suggesting it is undervalued. However, the P/E ratio, at 22.6x earnings, is above the US Food industry average, raising questions about potential multiple risk.

feed.title: BridgeBio Pharma stock hits all-time high at 78.69 USD
feed.summary: BridgeBio Pharma (BBIO) stock has reached an all-time high of $78.69, reflecting a 127.81% total return over the past year and a significant 65.35% surge in the last six months. The company, with a market capitalization of $15.08 billion, shows strong investor confidence despite a reported loss per share over the last year, attributed to robust performance of its flagship product Attruby and a healthy financial position with a current ratio of 3.88. Analysts anticipate continued sales growth and have issued optimistic price targets, with TD Cowen reiterating a Buy rating and Morgan Stanley initiating an Overweight rating.

feed.title: Empower Adds Blackstone to Bolster Private Market Investment Program
feed.summary: Empower Annuity Insurance Co. of America has added Blackstone Inc. to its alternative investment fund managers to expand private market investment offerings in its defined contribution plans. This move aims to provide millions of Americans with access to private investments, enhancing returns and diversification. Empower's platform allows employers to integrate private market collective investment trusts, with personalized allocations based on participants' profiles.

feed.title: The bearish pattern to watch on ConocoPhillips
feed.summary: ConocoPhillips (COP) has rallied over 13.5% since late November, but a technical analysis reveals a potentially bearish upsloping parallel channel on its daily chart. This pattern, characterized by controlled, narrowing price movement, often precedes a breakdown. Traders should watch for a break below the channel's lower boundary as a signal of weakening momentum and potential further downside for COP.

feed.title: Tesla reportedly struggling to offload inventory in India amid cancellations By Investing.com
feed.summary: Tesla is reportedly having difficulty selling about one-third of the initial Model Y vehicles imported to India last year, with many early reservation holders canceling orders. This struggle comes as Tesla faces declining global demand and was surpassed by BYD Co. as the top-selling EV manufacturer in 2025. To boost sales in India, where import taxes are high and prices start at nearly $70,000, Tesla is offering discounts of up to 200,000 rupees on certain Model Y variants.

feed.title: Bank Of New York Mellon Posts Solid Q4, Outlook Reflects More Durable EPS Growth
feed.summary: Bank of New York Mellon (NYSE: BK) reported strong fourth-quarter results, exceeding consensus for net interest income. Goldman Sachs reiterated a Buy rating and increased the price target to $143, noting the bank's "durable mid-teens % EPS growth" and accelerating organic growth. Management's conservative outlook suggests potential for upside, with a clear path to expand pre-tax margins and offer consistent capital returns.

feed.title: Kroger appoints new SVP of retail divisions
feed.summary: Kroger has promoted Victor Smith, former president of its Atlanta Division, to Senior Vice President of Retail Divisions. This move is part of several executive changes across the company, including other presidential appointments and retirements, as Kroger prepares to name a permanent CEO in Q1 2026. Smith, who started as a courtesy clerk in 1983, will work alongside two other senior VPs of retail divisions.

feed.title: QCOM vs. AMD: Which Semiconductor Stock is the Smarter Buy in 2026?
feed.summary: This article compares Qualcomm (QCOM) and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), two leading semiconductor firms, to determine which is a smarter investment for 2026. While Qualcomm focuses on 5G, AI, and diversified revenue streams, facing competition from Intel and Samsung, AMD is strengthening its AI capabilities and data center footprint, despite facing Intel in traditional computing and NVIDIA in GPUs. The analysis of Zacks estimates and valuation suggests Qualcomm might have a slight edge due to its more attractive valuation despite AMD's stronger recent price performance and consistent growth.

feed.title: Deutsche Bank Keeps a Hold Rating on General Mills, Inc. (GIS)
feed.summary: Deutsche Bank has reiterated its "Hold" rating on General Mills, Inc. (GIS) and raised its price target from $50 to $51. This decision follows General Mills' strong fiscal second-quarter earnings, which surpassed analyst expectations due to continued consumer preference for at-home eating. Despite a 7% drop in net sales to $4.86 billion, the company exceeded sales and profit projections, with adjusted results of $1.10 per share overcoming the $1.03 forecast.

feed.title: Analysts Offer Insights on Healthcare Companies: Boston Scientific (BSX), Regeneron (REGN) and Progyny (PGNY)
feed.summary: Three analysts recently provided bullish insights on several healthcare companies. Richard Newitter from Truist Financial maintained a Buy rating on Boston Scientific (BSX) with a strong buy consensus and a 32.2% upside. Gregory Renza, also from Truist Financial, maintained a Buy rating on Regeneron (REGN), which has a Moderate Buy consensus and a 6.0% upside. Jailendra Singh of Truist Financial kept a Buy rating on Progyny (PGNY), which holds a Strong Buy consensus with a 1.5% upside.

feed.title: Deutsche Bank Keeps a Hold Rating on General Mills, Inc. (GIS)
feed.summary: Deutsche Bank has maintained a Hold rating on General Mills, Inc. (GIS) and raised its price objective from $50 to $51 following the company's fiscal second-quarter earnings announcement. General Mills surpassed analyst estimates for sales and profit, with net sales falling 7% to $4.86 billion against a projected $4.78 billion, and adjusted earnings of $1.10 per share exceeding the $1.03 forecast. The company's performance was bolstered by sustained at-home eating trends, benefiting its breakfast cereal and pantry basic products.

feed.title: Wells Fargo Raises Price Target for Ecolab (ECL) to $275 | ECL S
feed.summary: Wells Fargo maintained an Equal-Weight rating on Ecolab (ECL) and raised its price target from $270 to $275, reflecting a 1.85% increase. This adjustment suggests continued confidence in Ecolab's market performance. Other recent analyst ratings for Ecolab have seen both increases and decreases in price targets, with the average target from 20 analysts suggesting a 7.20% upside from the current price, while GuruFocus estimates a 6.62% downside based on its GF Value.

feed.title: Stifel Raises Price Target for Teradyne (TER) to $270 Amid Buy Rating
feed.summary: Stifel has reaffirmed its Buy rating on Teradyne (TER) and increased its price target from $225 to $270, reflecting strong confidence in the company's financial performance and market potential. This latest upgrade follows a series of positive evaluations from other firms, including B of A Securities, Goldman Sachs, and Citigroup, which have also raised their price targets for TER. Teradyne, a provider of testing equipment for semiconductors and industrial automation robots, has seen its average analyst target price reach $208.11, while GuruFocus estimates a fair value of $149.29, indicating a potential downside from its current price.

feed.title: MSCI Receives Maintained Rating and Increased Price Target by We
feed.summary: Wells Fargo has maintained its "Equal-Weight" rating for MSCI and increased its price target from $570 to $590, a 3.51% rise. This decision reflects continued confidence in MSCI's market position and performance potential, following similar actions by other major financial institutions. Analysts' average one-year price target for MSCI is $647.63, suggesting a 9.84% upside from its current price.

feed.title: Vistra Outperforms Industry in the Past Month: How to Play the Stock?
feed.summary: Vistra Corp. (VST) has outperformed its industry in the past month, driven by its acquisition of Cogentrix Energy and a 20-year power purchase agreement with Meta to supply clean energy. The company's focus on clean energy, strategic investments, and strong asset base position it well for future growth amidst rising demand from AI-powered data centers. While VST shows strong estimated earnings growth and a high ROE, its current premium valuation suggests new investors might wait for a better entry point.

feed.title: IBM vs. MSFT: Which Cloud & AI Tech Giant is a Better Buy?
feed.summary: This article compares IBM (IBM) and Microsoft (MSFT) as leading players in the cloud computing and AI industries, evaluating which company presents a better investment opportunity. IBM is highlighted for its hybrid cloud and AI solutions, especially with the acquisition of HashiCorp and its watsonx platform, but faces competition and margin pressure. Microsoft's Azure platform is lauded for its market share, robust ecosystem, and strategic investments in OpenAI, although its escalating capital expenditures are noted as a concern.

feed.title: Wall Street Analysts Believe Uber (UBER) Could Rally 31.3%: Here's is How to Trade
feed.summary: Wall Street analysts project a potential 31.3% upside for Uber (UBER), with a mean price target of $112.14. While price targets are often viewed with skepticism, the strong agreement among analysts and positive earnings estimate revisions suggest a legitimate reason for optimism. Uber currently holds a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy), further indicating its near-term potential.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Says Cheesecake Factory Is “Way Too Cheap for a Very High-Quality Still Growth Company”
feed.summary: Jim Cramer believes Cheesecake Factory (NASDAQ: CAKE) is undervalued, calling it "way too cheap for a very high-quality still growth company" given its 13 times earnings valuation and 14% year-to-date gain. He highlighted the company's strong management, unique value proposition through its diverse menu, and impressive unit-level margins. Despite his positive sentiment, Cramer advised caution before buying, suggesting investors wait for upcoming earnings reports due to management's historically conservative outlook that has sometimes led to stock dips even after strong quarters.

feed.title: Jim Cramer on Kimberly-Clark: “I Think Michael Hsu Is Doing a Fantastic Job With What the Hand He’s Got”
feed.summary: Jim Cramer commented on Kimberly-Clark (NASDAQ: KMB), stating that CEO Michael Hsu is doing a "fantastic job" despite perceived weaknesses related to Kenvue and Procter & Gamble. He indicated that the stock, with a 5% yield and trading at 13 times earnings, seems intriguing. Cramer also discussed Kimberly-Clark's bold bid to acquire Kenvue, acknowledging the risks of lawsuits but noting the compelling value and potential for another acquirer to drive the stock up.

feed.title: MSCI (NYSE:MSCI) Stock Price Expected to Rise, Wells Fargo & Company Analyst Says
feed.summary: Wells Fargo & Company analysts increased their price target for MSCI (NYSE:MSCI) to $590, maintaining an "equal weight" rating, while the overall analyst consensus is a "Moderate Buy" with a $650.13 target. The company recently surpassed EPS expectations, announced a $3 billion share buyback, and reported significant insider buying by CEO Henry Fernandez. These factors, alongside positive ratings from other firms, suggest a potential upside for MSCI's stock.

feed.title: J.B. Hunt Transport Services (NASDAQ:JBHT) Price Target Raised to $189.00
feed.summary: Stifel Nicolaus has raised its price target for J.B. Hunt Transport Services (NASDAQ:JBHT) to $189.00 from $172.00, while maintaining a "hold" rating, suggesting a potential downside from its current trading price. Despite insider selling, institutional ownership remains high at nearly 75%, and the company recently reported strong quarterly earnings, beating analyst estimates. J.B. Hunt's stock has seen a significant price performance, trading near its 12-month high with a market cap of $19.46 billion.

feed.title: Wells Fargo & Company Issues Positive Forecast for FactSet Research Systems (NYSE:FDS) Stock Price
feed.summary: Wells Fargo & Company increased its price target for FactSet Research Systems (NYSE:FDS) stock from $260.00 to $265.00, despite maintaining an "underweight" rating, suggesting a potential downside. The article notes that FactSet recently exceeded quarterly EPS and revenue expectations, provided positive FY2026 EPS guidance, and authorized a $600 million share buyback program. Analyst consensus remains "Reduce" with an average price target of $313.00, while institutional investors have been adjusting their positions in the company.

feed.title: Wells Fargo & Company Lowers Equifax (NYSE:EFX) Price Target to $265.00
feed.summary: Wells Fargo & Company has lowered its price target for Equifax (NYSE:EFX) from $285.00 to $265.00, while maintaining an "overweight" rating. This adjustment still implies a potential upside of 21.36% from the company's previous close. Despite this reduction, analyst coverage remains largely positive, with a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating and an average price target of $273.07.

feed.title: Stifel Nicolaus Increases Teradyne (NASDAQ:TER) Price Target to $270.00
feed.summary: Stifel Nicolaus has increased its price target for Teradyne (NASDAQ:TER) from $225.00 to $270.00, maintaining a "buy" rating and suggesting an 18.66% upside. This new target is significantly above the consensus analyst target of $200.44 for the company. Teradyne recently reported strong quarterly earnings, beating expectations with an EPS of $0.85 and revenue of $769.2 million, and continues to attract attention from various institutional investors.

feed.title: Is Amazon’s Cloud Unit Big Enough to Reaccelerate Share Gains?
feed.summary: Amazon's cloud unit, Amazon Web Services (AWS), has seen its growth reaccelerate to 2022 levels, primarily driven by strong demand for artificial intelligence. CEO Andy Jassy highlighted the continued need for AI infrastructure and storage, which benefits AWS. The emergence of "physical AI" like humanoid robots and autonomous vehicles is expected to further boost cloud platform demand, making Amazon a promising stock for 2026 and beyond.

feed.title: Barclays Adjusts Southern Price Target to $88 From $91, Maintains Equal Weight Rating
feed.summary: Barclays has updated its price target for Southern Company (SO) to $88, down from $91, while reiterating an "Equal Weight" rating on the stock. Southern Company specializes in electricity production and distribution, with primary services including electricity distribution, natural gas distribution and marketing, and wholesale electricity sales.

feed.title: SLB Eyes Early Work In A US-Led Push Into Venezuela Oil
feed.summary: SLB (formerly Schlumberger) is positioned as a leading contender for contracts in Venezuela's oil sector if the US greenlights significant investment, with reports suggesting a potential $100 billion push. While rivals like Baker Hughes and Halliburton are also interested, SLB's existing presence and relationship with Chevron give it an advantage. The success of any ventures will largely hinge on US policy decisions, contract structures, and the ability to move resources into the country, rather than just engineering capabilities.
